The Dewey monument stands in the middle of Union Square in the heart of San Francisco. The nearly 25 m high granite Corinthian column is crowned by a statue of the Greek goddess of victory Nike.

The monument was erected in 1903 to commemorate the American victory under Admiral George Dewey over the Spanish at the Battle of Manila Bay (1898). Today it is the distinctive landmark of Union Square and a popular meeting and orientation point in the bustling city centre.

I've spent a lot of time sitting in Union Square killing time.  Lots of people around always.  And I gazed at this monument to the admiral from the 1812 War.  Not something that I easily relate to, but the obelisk is a pleasant addition to this central city park.
